description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has just received three log files that were captured during a production incident.  
Your objective is to produce two machine-readable diagnostic artefacts that will later be consumed by an automated incident-analysis pipeline.

STARTING SITUATION  
1. A directory containing raw service logs already exists:  
   • /home/user/logs/webapp1.log  
   • /home/user/logs/webapp2.log  
   • /home/user/logs/dbservice.log  
2. Each log line is guaranteed to follow this exact pattern (including the trailing closing square bracket, a single space and then the message body):  
   [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] MESSAGE_TEXT  
   Example:  
   [2023-11-04 09:17:10] ERROR ERR101 Failed query  

REQUIRED END STATE  
Create a new directory called /home/user/diagnostics and inside that directory generate the following two files:

A) /home/user/diagnostics/summary_report.txt – a plain-text summary of high-level statistics for ALL three input log files.  
   1. The file MUST start with the exact header line:  
      SUMMARY REPORT  
   2. After the header, write one block per source log, in the same order the files are listed above.  
   3. Each block MUST contain exactly 7 lines, using the precise keys and the formatting shown below (values are examples – your script must calculate the real numbers):  

      LOG: /home/user/logs/webapp1.log  
      TOTAL_LINES: 123  
      ERROR_LINES: 12  
      WARN_LINES: 4  
      LAST_ERROR: [2023-11-04 09:20:05] ERROR ERR102 Timeout calling API  
      FIRST_TIMESTAMP: 2023-11-04 09:15:23  
      LAST_TIMESTAMP: 2023-11-04 09:20:05  

      An empty line must be inserted **after** each 7-line block (including after the last block) so automated parsers can split the report easily.

   Notes on how to compute the values:  
   • TOTAL_LINES            – count every line in the file.  
   • ERROR_LINES            – lines that contain the substring “ERROR”.  
   • WARN_LINES             – lines that contain the substring “WARN”.  
   • LAST_ERROR             – the complete text of the final (“bottom-most”) line containing the substring “ERROR”.  
   • FIRST_TIMESTAMP / LAST_TIMESTAMP – extract the timestamp portion (without brackets) from the very first and the very last line in the file respectively.

B) /home/user/diagnostics/errors.json – a JSON object that lists every unique error code that appears across **all** three input logs and how many times it occurs globally.  
   • Error codes are three-letter strings that start with “ERR” followed immediately by three digits (e.g. ERR101).  
   • The JSON must be un-pretty-printed (i.e. a single line, no whitespace except the required commas and braces).  
   • Keys MUST be sorted in lexicographic ascending order.  
   Example format (values below are only illustrative):

   {"ERR001":2,"ERR014":7,"ERR999":1}

CONSTRAINTS & HINTS  
• You do not have root access, therefore everything you create has to live under /home/user.  
• Assume the diagnostics directory does **not** yet exist; create it if necessary.  
• Use whatever shell tools you like (awk, grep, jq, parallel, xargs, tee, etc.). Feel free to launch multiple background jobs if that helps you compute faster; the test harness cares only about the final contents of the two required files, not about how you got there.  
• Make sure the numeric counts and timestamps in summary_report.txt and the error-code counts in errors.json are all correct — these values will be checked by an automated test.  
• Do not change, delete or move the original log files; only read them.  
• When you are finished you should be able to run the following commands and see exactly the shown paths (their *contents* will be graded automatically):  

  ls -1 /home/user/diagnostics  
    errors.json  
    summary_report.txt  

Any deviation from the specified file names, order, or formatting will cause the automated grader to mark the task as failed.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

/home/user/logs/webapp1.log
[2023-11-04 09:15:23] INFO User login id=23
[2023-11-04 09:16:00] WARN Session delay
[2023-11-04 09:17:10] ERROR ERR101 Failed query
[2023-11-04 09:19:30] INFO Data saved
[2023-11-04 09:20:05] ERROR ERR102 Timeout calling API

/home/user/logs/webapp2.log
[2023-11-04 09:12:00] INFO Service started
[2023-11-04 09:13:34] ERROR ERR201 Missing config
[2023-11-04 09:14:00] WARN Cache cold
[2023-11-04 09:15:45] ERROR ERR201 Missing config
[2023-11-04 09:18:50] INFO Healthcheck OK
[2023-11-04 09:19:59] ERROR ERR202 Disk full

/home/user/logs/dbservice.log
[2023-11-04 09:10:10] INFO Connection pool created
[2023-11-04 09:11:12] ERROR ERR301 Connection refused
[2023-11-04 09:14:50] WARN Slow query detected
[2023-11-04 09:16:30] ERROR ERR301 Connection refused
[2023-11-04 09:18:00] ERROR ERR302 Deadlock found

Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

/home/user/diagnostics/summary_report.txt
SUMMARY REPORT
LOG: /home/user/logs/webapp1.log
TOTAL_LINES: 5
ERROR_LINES: 2
WARN_LINES: 1
LAST_ERROR: [2023-11-04 09:20:05] ERROR ERR102 Timeout calling API
FIRST_TIMESTAMP: 2023-11-04 09:15:23
LAST_TIMESTAMP: 2023-11-04 09:20:05

LOG: /home/user/logs/webapp2.log
TOTAL_LINES: 6
ERROR_LINES: 3
WARN_LINES: 1
LAST_ERROR: [2023-11-04 09:19:59] ERROR ERR202 Disk full
FIRST_TIMESTAMP: 2023-11-04 09:12:00
LAST_TIMESTAMP: 2023-11-04 09:19:59

LOG: /home/user/logs/dbservice.log
TOTAL_LINES: 5
ERROR_LINES: 3
WARN_LINES: 1
LAST_ERROR: [2023-11-04 09:18:00] ERROR ERR302 Deadlock found
FIRST_TIMESTAMP: 2023-11-04 09:10:10
LAST_TIMESTAMP: 2023-11-04 09:18:00


/home/user/diagnostics/errors.json
{"ERR101":1,"ERR102":1,"ERR201":2,"ERR202":1,"ERR301":2,"ERR302":1}
name: task_000000_4bca03d7
